cases.pain: Small family-run coffee business needed more efficient workforce management while staying lean.
cases.outcome: Progressed from basic clock-ins to fuller timesheet tracking and better workforce productivity insight.
cases.name_drop: Coffee Break (SG heritage coffee, 3 stalls): clock-ins to timesheet visibility for lean F&B ops.

cases.pain: Fast expansion made multi-location scheduling and spreadsheets too time-consuming.
cases.outcome: Scheduling became easier and admin burden dropped, giving management more time for growth work.
cases.name_drop: L'Osteria (ID pizza, 5 locations/300 staff): multi-location scheduling moved off spreadsheets.

cases.pain: Family-owned F&B group needed consistency and efficiency across a growing outlet team.
cases.outcome: StaffAny supported more consistent workforce management across outlets and back-office HR/ops work.
cases.name_drop: Mr Fish (MY family F&B, 100+ staff): consistency across outlets and back-office workforce ops.

cases.pain: Punch-card attendance made multi-location staff tracking and real-time visibility difficult.
cases.outcome: Attendance and performance tracking improved staff allocation and operational control.
cases.name_drop: Retrofit (SG rehab/fitness): punch-card limits solved with attendance and staff-allocation visibility.

cases.pain: Messy Excel rostering and documents caused attendance visibility gaps and overtime payroll pain.
cases.outcome: Scheduling, attendance, real-time reports, and PayrollAny helped manage punctuality, overtime, and payroll processing.
cases.name_drop: The Wine Company (SG bistro): Excel rostering and overtime pain handled with HRMS, attendance, and PayrollAny.

cases.pain: Managers used WhatsApp and Google Forms for manual scheduling while operating through Covid restrictions.
cases.outcome: Scheduling became easier and managers spent less time juggling manual rosters.
cases.name_drop: True Breakfast (SG F&B, 2 branches): WhatsApp/Google Forms scheduling replaced by easier roster management.

cases.pain: WhatsApp availability gathering and weekly scheduling created manual work and paperwork.
cases.outcome: Scheduling and attendance transparency improved productivity, accuracy, and trust around timesheets.
cases.name_drop: Ya Kun (SG heritage chain, 71 outlets): WhatsApp scheduling moved toward transparent attendance/timesheets.

cases.pain: Scaling from 2 to 6 outlets exposed WhatsApp and Excel scheduling limits.
cases.outcome: Scheduling and attendance support helped the brand save time and labour cost while scaling.
cases.name_drop: Bober Tea (SG bubble tea, 6 outlets): scaling exposed WhatsApp/Excel limits, then improved scheduling control.

cases.pain: Pen-and-paper time and attendance was inefficient and time-consuming.
cases.outcome: Onsite time clock improved time tracking accuracy and scheduling productivity.
cases.name_drop: Fight Zone (SG fitness, 3 outlets): pen-and-paper attendance improved through onsite time clock.

cases.pain: Punch-card and biometric hardware created repair, maintenance, and attendance-cheating costs.
cases.outcome: Digital clock-in/out removed device maintenance burden and gave real-time attendance checks.
cases.name_drop: YC Enterprise (BN automotive, 40+ staff): moved from costly biometric/punch-card hardware to digital timeclock.

cases.pain: Needed a digital solution with automated scheduling, timesheets, and attendance as the business expanded.
cases.outcome: StaffAny provided a cleaner digital alternative for scheduling, timesheets, and attendance tracking.
cases.name_drop: The Cat Cafe (SG cafe, 2 outlets): expansion pushed them from manual ops to digital scheduling/timesheets.

cases.pain: Manual biometric attendance caused hardware failures, payroll gaps, and manager timesheet checks.
cases.outcome: Operational efficiency improved and punctuality issues dropped by 30-40%.
cases.name_drop: Dimbulah Coffee (SG, 13 outlets): biometric issues fixed, with 30-40% fewer punctuality issues.

cases.pain: Growing kiosk headcount made communication and scheduling increasingly time-consuming.
cases.outcome: Scheduling time dropped from about 3 hours to 1 hour, with QR attendance and team communication support.
cases.name_drop: Foreword Coffee (SG, 4 outlets): scheduling reduced from about 3 hours to 1 hour with QR attendance.

cases.pain: Paper timesheets, punch cards, and WhatsApp photos made month-end consolidation painful and exposed time theft.
cases.outcome: Processing moved to about 3 man-days and the case estimates about $2,000 monthly savings.
cases.name_drop: Kiosks Collective (SG, 65+ outlets/500 staff): paper timesheets to 3 man-days, about $2k monthly savings.

cases.pain: Manual books and Excel made monthly hours, payroll, and scheduling stressful.
cases.outcome: Payroll checking dropped from about half a day to roughly one hour, with easier availability and scheduling.
cases.name_drop: Mission Juice (SG juicery): payroll checking moved from about half a day to roughly one hour.

cases.pain: Punch cards allowed manipulation of clock-in/out records and created overtime control problems.
cases.outcome: Auto-clock-out and digital attendance improved overtime control for 24-hour station operations.
cases.name_drop: Petron Grandsaga (MY petrol, 24-hour ops): punch-card OT leakage controlled with auto-clock-out.

cases.pain: Covid demand surge forced faster roster planning, contactless time tracking, and cross-department coordination.
cases.outcome: Digital rostering and time tracking reduced planning steps and improved manpower allocation.
cases.name_drop: Phoon Huat (SG retail, 15+ outlets): Covid demand surge handled with digital rostering/time tracking.

cases.pain: Older staff with faded thumbprints struggled with biometric clock-in.
cases.outcome: Mobile clock-in/out replaced biometric friction and the case frames savings up to half a manager salary.
cases.name_drop: Tea Tree Cafe (SG, 10 outlets): biometric thumbprint friction replaced with mobile clock-in/out.

cases.pain: Heavy reliance on Google Sheets and links created scheduling and communication gaps.
cases.outcome: Scheduling became easier and team communication/transparency improved.
cases.name_drop: The Royals Bistro (SG halal F&B): Google Sheets scheduling gaps improved with StaffAny scheduling.

cases.pain: Collecting punch cards and calculating hours across six outlets took nearly two days and delayed attendance visibility.
cases.outcome: Scheduling and real-time attendance helped HR manage six outlets in one platform; announcements reduced outlet visits.
cases.name_drop: Belly & The Chef (MY F&B, 6 outlets): punch-card collection to one-platform scheduling and real-time attendance.

cases.pain: Manual processes bogged down operations across indoor activity centers.
cases.outcome: Scheduling digitalisation made staff schedule management easier.
cases.name_drop: DreamUs Group (SG indoor playgrounds, 4 centers): manual ops digitalised through scheduling.

cases.pain: Old system had access restrictions, slow web workflows, hardware needs, and manual HR admin.
cases.outcome: Centralised data, automated scheduling, attendance, leave, and reporting helped streamline HR operations.
cases.name_drop: Keisuke (SG Japanese chain): old system/hardware pain improved with centralised HR data and automated scheduling.

cases.pain: Manual staff-management processes were tedious and time-consuming for a new F&B concept.
cases.outcome: Automation improved efficiency and productivity in staff management.
cases.name_drop: The Kongsee (SG restaurant/bar): manual staff-management processes improved through automation.

cases.pain: Paper punch cards and manual Excel entries made payroll accuracy and work-hour tracking difficult.
cases.outcome: Automated time tracking improved payroll accuracy and the case estimates about 15% labour-cost savings.
cases.name_drop: Paan KL (MY restaurant): paper/Excel time tracking moved to automated attendance, about 15% labour-cost saving.

cases.pain: Biometric thumbprint clock-in, WhatsApp scheduling, and paper timesheets made daily changes and month-end checks hard.
cases.outcome: StaffAny made ad hoc schedule changes easier and helped track latecomers and no-shows.
cases.name_drop: PastaGo (SG pasta, 4 locations): biometric/WhatsApp scheduling pain improved with ad hoc roster changes and no-show tracking.

cases.pain: Scheduling and payroll for 220 staff across outlets took days using manual methods.
cases.outcome: Manual scheduling/payroll work became a faster, more efficient HR process across Braud locations.
cases.name_drop: Braud Group (ID bakery/cafe, 3 outlets/220 staff): scheduling/payroll went from days of manual work to faster HR ops.

cases.size: multiple concepts: farm, restaurant, pilot kitchen, fish-soup stall tests
cases.pain: Concept-specific staffing, food cost, rent, manpower, and OT visibility cannot be managed by one flat labour benchmark.
cases.outcome: StaffAny is cited for easier clock-in/out, pay visibility, and OT overshoot control.
cases.name_drop: Ah Hua Kelong / Scaled: concept-specific labour targets plus StaffAny clock-in/out, pay, and OT visibility.
